summary:
The most likely diagnosis given the history and imaging findings is medial tibial stress syndrome (shin splint), an overuse condition caused by repeated mechanical stress on the tibia’s periosteum. This explains persistent anteromedial pain despite negative radiographs, making it consistent with typical athletic-related pathology.
